excerpt: Table 2 lists 473,920 residents in the 1991 prefecture population.
transform_note: Stored as 1991-01-01 because the table cites the 1991 census year rather than an exact day.
confidence_note: Strong administrative baseline.

excerpt: The author’s growth note says the prefecture probably reached about 500,000 by March 1994.
transform_note: Approximate author extrapolation using 3% annual growth from the 1991 census.
confidence_note: Useful as a denominator anchor but inherently approximate.

excerpt: Table 2 reports 71,225 Tutsi in the 1991 prefecture population.
transform_note: 1991 census year stored as 1991-01-01.
confidence_note: Census-classification caveats still apply, but this is the direct local baseline used by the author.

excerpt: Verwimp reports 59,050 identified genocide victims in Kibuye.
transform_note: Treated as a strict named/registered-victim floor, not the full civilian death total.
confidence_note: Strongest local direct-death source in the bundle, but the author explicitly says it is an underestimate.

victim_side: Hutu civilians allied by marriage to Tutsi or opposed to the Habyarimana regime
excerpt: The Kibuye register omitted an undetermined number of Hutu civilians killed for marital or political reasons.
transform_note: No attempt made to force a numeric estimate from this qualitative statement.
confidence_note: Important omission from the Tutsi-centered registry.

excerpt: Verwimp gives a survivor interval of roughly 10,000-15,000 Tutsi.
transform_note: Stored as a source interval; no source-best point is supplied.
confidence_note: Useful for a transparent derived civilian death bound.

victim_side: Tutsi civilians in Bisesero, Kibuye Prefecture
excerpt: The paper’s preferred Bisesero figure is 13,000 direct deaths.
transform_note: Author’s preferred Bisesero estimate based on commune-by-commune reconstruction within the Ibuka file.
confidence_note: Useful auxiliary hotspot figure, not the full-prefecture numerator.

excerpt: The main killing wave in Kibuye was concentrated in the first two weeks.
transform_note: This is a share/timing claim, used to justify a short, intense Kibuye episode rather than a whole-country denominator.
confidence_note: Strong temporal concentration, but still based on the same underlying registry reconstruction.

inflicting_side: soldiers, police, Interahamwe, and civilians directed by Kibuye authorities
excerpt: ICTR legal findings explicitly name the mix of soldiers, police, Interahamwe, and civilians attacking Tutsi in Kibuye.
transform_note: Used for side attribution rather than for a numeric death count.
confidence_note: High confidence on attacker identity for the named Kibuye sites in this case.

excerpt: Local administrative data imply 17.5% Tutsi in Gikongoro.
transform_note: Local 1990 administrative share, not the 1991 census share.
confidence_note: Central to Verpoorten’s under-reporting argument.

excerpt: The official 1991 census put Gikongoro’s Tutsi share at 12.8%.
transform_note: Stored as 1991-01-01 because the source discusses the census year rather than an exact day.
confidence_note: Used mainly to show the size of the under-reporting gap.

excerpt: Verpoorten’s headline result is a 25% Tutsi survival rate in Gikongoro.
confidence_note: Model-based prefectural average rather than a named-victim registry outcome.

excerpt: Table 7 gives male Tutsi death shares across models D-F.
transform_note: Best point left null because the source reports three model-specific values rather than a single preferred point.
confidence_note: Useful sex-specific auxiliary metric only.

excerpt: Table 7 gives female Tutsi death shares across models D-F.
transform_note: Best point left null because the source reports three model-specific values rather than a single preferred point.
confidence_note: Useful sex-specific auxiliary metric only.

excerpt: HRW places about 7,000 regular troops in Kigali and nearby areas in early April.
pages_or_sections: Section 'April 1994: The Month That Would Not End'
transform_note: Pre-episode positioning context for the Kigali opening phase.
confidence_note: Useful as a city-level force concentration clue, not a full person-month denominator.

excerpt: HRW reports roughly 2,000 militia already in place in Kigali before the killing began.
pages_or_sections: Section 'April 1994: The Month That Would Not End'
confidence_note: Important for classifying Kigali as a mixed military-civilian urban episode.

excerpt: HRW notes the formal 600-man RPF CND contingent plus covert reinforcements.
pages_or_sections: Section 'April 1994: The Month That Would Not End'
transform_note: Only the formal 600 is numeric; covert reinforcements are qualitative in this source.
confidence_note: Useful lower-bound military presence for the Kigali episode.

excerpt: McDoom’s preferred national Tutsi death range is 491k-522k.
transform_note: Source uses 6 April start; retained as-is for national context.
confidence_note: Contextual national benchmark only.

excerpt: McDoom cites HRW/Des Forges and Gersony lower-bound ranges for Hutu civilians killed by the RPA.
transform_note: Davenport and Stam’s higher figure of up to 80,000 is noted by McDoom but kept outside this claim because the source treats the lower-bound figures separately and documentation remains poor.
confidence_note: National contextual claim only; not used in subnational DR estimation.

victim_side: FAR and RPF/RPA combatants in the Rwandan civil war
excerpt: Cunningham gives a national direct-fighting combatant death total of 7,500.
transform_note: War-wide national figure retained as context only; not transported into prefectural episodes.
confidence_note: Snippet-level access here means this should be treated as contextual rather than definitive.

excerpt: Tripodi places FAR strength at 5,000-6,000 when the war began.
transform_note: Best point is the midpoint of the stated source range.
confidence_note: National context only.

excerpt: Tripodi reports a combined army-plus-gendarmerie force of about 40,000 by the war’s later phase.
transform_note: National ceiling only; unsuitable as a direct prefectural denominator.
confidence_note: Broad national context.

excerpt: Tripodi says the gendarmerie expanded from roughly 2,000 to 6,000.
transform_note: Broad institutional context for organized-armed personnel.
confidence_note: National context only.

victim_side: Moderate political leaders and Tutsi civilians in Kigali
inflicting_side: Presidential Guard, supported by the para-commando and reconnaissance battalions
excerpt: Tripodi identifies the elite FAR units leading the initial Kigali killing.
transform_note: Side-attribution support for the Kigali candidate episode.
confidence_note: Strong contextual attribution, but not a death count.

excerpt: Armstrong et al. show how broad national casualty totals vary sharply with source weighting.
transform_note: Retained as mixed casualty context only and excluded from the core direct-death estimate path.
confidence_note: Useful only to show disagreement and why mechanical averaging is inappropriate.
